{"code": "import json
# Categorize the articles
def categorize_article(title, description):
    text = (title + ' ' + description).lower()
    
    # Science/Technology keywords
    science_tech_keywords = [
        'science', 'technology', 'tech', 'research', 'laboratory', 'experiment',
        'energy', 'electricity', 'device', 'machine', 'innovation', 'game',
        'computer', 'software', 'internet', 'digital', 'data', 'algorithm',
        'medical', 'health', 'genetic', 'dna', 'clinical', 'physics',
        'chemistry', 'biology', 'mathematics', 'engineering', 'robotics',
        'nano', 'biotech', 'space', 'satellite', 'rocket', 'astronomy',
        'ai', 'artificial intelligence', 'mobile', 'smartphone', 'app',
        'electric', 'battery', 'telecom', 'network', 'cyber', 'security'
    ]
    
    # Sports keywords
    sports_keywords = [
        'football', 'baseball', 'basketball', 'soccer', 'hockey', 'tennis',
        'olympics', 'championship', 'tournament', 'coach', 'player', 'team',
        'game', 'win', 'score', 'record', 'season', 'league', 'coach',
        'quarterback', 'broncos', 'practice'  # From the sample
    ]
    
    # Business keywords
    business_keywords = [
        'business', 'economy', 'market', 'stock', 'profit', 'loss',
        'company', 'corporation', 'investment', 'trade', 'financial',
        'bank', 'retail', 'sales', 'revenue', 'earnings', 'shares',
        'ceo', 'executive', 'deal', 'merger', 'acquisition', 'prices',
        'bhp', 'billiton', 'mining'  # From the sample
    ]
    
    # World keywords (general news)
    world_keywords = [
        'world', 'global', 'international', 'countries', 'nations',
        'politics', 'government', 'president', 'minister', 'election',
        'war', 'peace', 'conflict', 'united nations', 'foreign',
        'diplomatic', 'treaty', 'china'  # From the sample
    ]
    
    # Check for Science/Technology
    if any(keyword in text for keyword in science_tech_keywords):
        return 'Science/Technology'
    
    # Check for Sports
    if any(keyword in text for keyword in sports_keywords):
        return 'Sports'
    
    # Check for Business
    if any(keyword in text for keyword in business_keywords):
        return 'Business'
    
    # Check for World
    if any(keyword in text for keyword in world_keywords):
        return 'World'
    
    # Default category (most articles will fit somewhere)
    return 'World'

# Sample articles from previous query
samples = [
    {"title": "GameBoy mini-games win prize", "description": "A set of GameBoy micro-games is named as the most innovative game of the year at a festival in Scotland."},
    {"title": "Bailey Tries WR", "description": "Pro Bowl cornerback Champ Bailey practiced with the offense at wide reciever during the Denver Broncos' practice on Tuesday."},
    {"title": "Students Win \$100,000 in National Team Science Competition", "description": "Lucie Guo, motivated by the death of her grandfather in China before she was born, spent two summers doing research in a Duke University laboratory."},
    {"title": "Energy from waves  teenager wins science award", "description": "A teenager from the San Diego, California area has won The Siemens Westinghouse Competition in Math, Science and Technology for his  quot;Gyro-Gen, quot; a machine that produces electricity from ocean waves."},
    {"title": "China #39;s appetite boosts BHP", "description": "BHP Billiton, the world #39;s biggest mining company, has doubled its profits in the second half of the year on the back of booming global commodity prices. "}
]

# Test categorization
categories = []
for article in samples:
    cat = categorize_article(article['title'], article['description'])
    categories.append(cat)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(categories))"}

amy_articles = locals()['var_functions.query_db:26']

def categorize_article(title, description):
    text = (title + ' ' + description).lower()
    
    # Science/Technology keywords

        'science', 'technology', 'tech', 'research', 'laboratory', 'experiment',
        'energy', 'electricity', 'device', 'machine', 'innovation', 'game',
        'computer', 'software', 'internet', 'digital', 'data', 'algorithm',

        'ai', 'artificial intelligence', 'mobile', 'smartphone', 'app',
        'electric', 'battery', 'telecom', 'network', 'cyber', 'security',
        'television', 'tv', 'gaming', 'video game', 'micro-games', 'gameboy'
    ]
    
    # Check for Science/Technology
    if any(keyword in text for keyword in science_tech_keywords):
        return 'Science/Technology'
    
    # For now, if not Science/Technology, mark as 'Other'  
    return 'Other'

# Categorize the 5 articles we have

{"code": "import json

# We'll use the full results from different queries
# Let's summarize what we have:

# From query_db:2, we have 111 article IDs for Amy Jones
amy_article_ids_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:2']
amy_article_ids = sorted([int(item['article_id']) for item in amy_article_ids_data])

# From query_db:22, we have all articles in the collection
full_articles_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:22']
with open(full_articles_file, 'r') as f:
    all_articles = json.load(f)

existing_article_ids = sorted([int(article['article_id']) for article in all_articles])

# Find which Amy Jones articles actually exist
existing_amy_articles = []
for article in all_articles:
    article_id = int(article['article_id'])
    if article_id in amy_article_ids:
        existing_amy_articles.append(article)

# Categorize the articles that exist
def categorize_article(title, description):
    text = (title + ' ' + description).lower()
    
    science_tech_keywords = [
        'science', 'technology', 'tech', 'research', 'laboratory', 'experiment',
        'energy', 'electricity', 'device', 'machine', 'innovation', 'game',
        'computer', 'software', 'internet', 'digital', 'data', 'algorithm',
        'medical', 'health', 'genetic', 'dna', 'clinical', 'physics',
        'chemistry', 'biology', 'mathematics', 'engineering', 'robotics',
        'nano', 'biotech', 'space', 'satellite', 'rocket', 'astronomy'
    ]
    
    if any(keyword in text for keyword in science_tech_keywords):
        return 'Science/Technology'
    
    return 'Not Science/Technology'

results = []
for article in existing_amy_articles:
    category = categorize_article(article['title'], article['description'])
    results.append({
        'article_id': article['article_id'],
        'title': article['title'],
        'category': category
    })

sci_tech_count = sum(1 for r in results if r['category'] == 'Science/Technology')
total_count = len(results)

fraction = sci_tech_count / total_count if total_count > 0 else 0

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'metadata_article_count': len(amy_article_ids),
    'actual_article_count': len(existing_amy_articles),
    'categorized_results': results,
    'science_technology_count': sci_tech_count,
    'total_analyzed': total_count,
    'fraction': fraction
}))"}
